title: Cyathocalyx deltoideus
intro: Cyathocalyx deltoideus, also known as the deltoid Cyathocalyx, is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyathocalyx deltoideus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cyathocalyx deltoideus is classified under the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family consists of about 130 genera and over 2,000 species. The Annonaceae family is part of the order Magnoliales and the subclass Magnoliidae. The classification of Cyathocalyx deltoideus is further detailed in its classification as a species of the genus Cyathocalyx. The genus Cyathocalyx itself belongs to the family Annonaceae.
